title: Strict OOF Blend With Post-Hoc Calibration
summary: Different objectives can be complementary: binary classifiers estimate marginal stop risk, rankers estimate within-lap ordering, and regime experts capture heterogeneity.
rationale: Different objectives can be complementary: binary classifiers estimate marginal stop risk, rankers estimate within-lap ordering, and regime experts capture heterogeneity. Calibration docs and stacking literature both stress that combination should use disjoint out-of-fold predictions.
implementation_hint: Build OOF predictions from the current best classifier, the lap-group ranker, and the regime expert/CatBoost model. Train a tiny logistic or isotonic/sigmoid/temperature calibrator only on OOF predictions, then refit base models on full train and apply the learned combiner.
expected_effect: Can convert model diversity into a small but real AUC gain while improving submission probability shape.
risk: If the base models are too correlated, stacking adds variance without lift; isotonic can overfit on small calibration slices.
